rationale: Audience overlap is strong — "relaxed sorting" hooks index well with the casual 25-55 female puzzle demo Marble Sort! targets, and the satisfaction emotional pitch matches the game's calm/satisfying UI mood. However, there's clear friction: the bus/passenger color-sort metaphor is a different IP entirely and showing it risks bait-and-switch (low mechanic fidelity to the actual marble-on-conveyor physics loop), plus the member palette (steel blue + coral red) clashes with the game's pastel lavender + orange identity. Recommend adapting the hook structure (hand + stylus + "actually relaxing?" VO) but reshooting with real Marble Sort! gameplay and recoloring overlays to the lavender/orange brand before scaling spend.
